original: A West German court rules that impostor Anna Anderson failed to prove that she was missing Russian duchess Anastasia Romanov, ending a legal case that lasted almost 30 years

British television programme "That's Life!" surprises guest Nicholas Winton with an audience full of grown-up children that he saved from German-occupied Czechoslovakia, bringing them to safety in the UK
Francis CrickandJames Watsondiscover the chemical structure of the DNA molecule (double-helix polymer) using X-ray diffraction studies developed byRosalind FranklinandMaurice Wilkins
Oscar Grundén skates world record 500m (50.8 sec)
Karl Schäferof Austria follows up his Winter Olympics victory with his 7th consecutive men’s figure skating World Championship title in Paris, France
Launch of Discoverer 1 (WTR)-1st polar orbit
Iolanda Chen triple jumps world indoor record hop step (14.46m)
Pluto-observing spacecraft New Horizons flies by Jupiter
Home team United States wins its first Olympic ice hockey gold medal at Squaw Valley; with 9-4 win over Czechoslovakia
1st killer whale born in captivity (Marineland, Los Angeles California)
Alexei Ekimov, Russian-American physicist (2023 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for discovery of quantum dots), born in Soviet Union[1]
Ladby Ship is discovered within a Viking grave on the island of Funen in Denmark by amateur archaeologist Poul Helweg Mikkelsen
Caroline Walker runs world female record marathon (3:02:53)
US 65th manned space mission STS 36 (Atlantis 6) launches into orbit
Mark Pavelich, American ice hockey forward (Olympic gold 1980 'Miracle on Ice'; NY Rangers, Minnesota North Stars, San Jose Sharks), born in Eveleth, Minnesota (d. 2021)
World War II: Operation Biting - British RAF drops 119 paratroopers near village of Bruneval, France, where the capture a German radar station and two technicians
Calvin Murphy (Hou), sets NBA record with 78 consecutive free throws
First flight of the RQ-4 Global Hawk, the first unmanned aerial vehicle certified to file its own flight plans and fly regularly in US civilian airspace
John Tenniel, English cartoonist and illustrator (Punch, Alice in Wonderland), born in London, England (d. 1914)
Wilt Chamberlainsinks NBA record 35th consecutive field goal
Coronation ceremony of Liu Bang as Emperor Gaozu of Han takes place, initiating four centuries of the Han Dynasty's rule over China
Biela's Comet rediscovered by Austrian astronomer Wilhelm von Biela (originally discovered 1772)
Edward Acheson of Pennsylvania, patents an abrasive he names "carborundum" (Silicon carbide)
1st televised basketball game (U of Pitts beats Fordham U, 50-37)
Arrests of the ten-Boom family in Nazi occupied Netherlands (Haarlem) through a Dutch collaborator on charges of hiding Jews
Pirate Radio Hauraki, on a boat floating off coast of NZ, returns to the air
British satirical puppet show "Spitting Image" premieres on ITV
Nisqually Earthquake measuring 6.8 on the Richter scale hits Nisqually Valley and the Seattle, Tacoma, and Olympia areas of Washington State
Charles Darwin, aboard HMS Beagle, arrives in the town of Salvador in the Brazilian state of Bahia
PGA Championship Men's Golf, PGA National East:Jack Nicklausleads wire-to-wire for his second career Grand Slam, winning by two strokes overBilly Casper
1st boat load of gold rush prospectors arrives in San Francisco from the east coast
Henry the Young King, son of Henry II of England, crowned aged 15 during his father's lifetime, born in London (d. 1183)
Guillaume Delisle, French cartographer known for his accurate maps of the Americas including Louisiana and Mississippi River, born in Paris (d. 1726)
Karl Earnest von Baer, Estonian-German embryologist (founding father of embryology), born in Jerwen County, Governate of Estonia, Russian Empire (d. 1876)
Rocky Dijon [Kwasi Dzidzornu] Ghanaian rock and R&B percussionist (The Rolling Stones, Taj Mahal, Stevie Wonder), born in British Gold Coast colony, Africa (d. 1993)
Cindy Wilson, American rock vocalist (B-52's - "Love Shack"), born in Athens, Georgia
Dr Elias L"nnrot publishes Finnish poem "Kalevala"
Wallace Carothers manufactures 1st nylon polymer
The erroneous word "Dord" is discovered in the Webster's New International Dictionary, Second Edition, prompting an investigation
American engineer Wright Forrester issued a patent for computer core memory
Cavern Club (Beatles hangout) in Liverpool, England closes
Over 1 million Taiwanese participate in the 228 Hand-in-Hand Rally, forming a 500-kilometer (300-mile) long human chain to commemorate the 228 Incident in 1947
Final TV episode of "M*A*S*H" airs on CBS, a two-hour special directed by series starAlan Aldatitled "Goodbye, Farewell, and Amen"; a record 125 million viewers watch in the US
Mary Lyon, American educator and activist for woman's higher education (set up and first Principal of Mt Holyoke Female Seminary), born near Buckland, Massachusetts (d. 1849)
Milton Caniff, American cartoonist 'Rembrandt of the Comic Strip' (Terry & Pirates), born in Hillsboro, Ohio (d. 1988)
Peter Medawar, British biologist (1960 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine "for discovery of acquired immunological tolerance"), born in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil (d. 1987)
Svend Asmussen, Danish jazz violinist, known as "The Fiddling Viking" (The Swe-Danes), born in Copenhagen, Denmark (d. 2017)
Leon Cooper, American physicist who co-developed the BCS theory of superconductivity (Nobel 1972), born in the Bronx, New York (d. 2024)
Gavin MacLeod [Allan See], American actor (The Mary Tyler Moore Show - "Murray"; The Love Boat - "Captain Stubing"; McHale's Navy - "Happy"), born in Mt Kisco, New York (d. 2021)
Ysaye Maria Barnwell, American singer gospel and a cappella singer (Sweet Honey in the Rock, 1979-2013), composer, and producer, born in Harlem, New York City
-Mar 3] 3 Day Sea battle English beat Dutch
Frenchman Elias Neau opens a school for blacks in NYC
Pope Clement XIII allows Bible to be translated into various languages
1st commercial railroad in US, Baltimore & Ohio (B&O) chartered
US forces numbering less than 1,000 defeat a larger Mexico force in the Battle of the Sacramento River

1st National Woman's Day is observed in the United States. Organized by the Socialist Party of America in honor of the 1908 garment workers' strike in New York, where women protested against working conditions.
Construction begins on Tower of Jewels for the San Francisco Exposition
Canadian Rugby Union adopts the forward pass
Pat Verbeek becomes 1st NJ Devil to score 4 goals in an NHL game
Nevada-Semipalatinsk anti-nuclear movement founded by Olzhas Suleimenov in Kazakhstan, initially aiming to close Semipalatinsk nuclear test site (closed 1991)
